references/eClassifiers/StateResource/in.title: StateResource
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/in.content: A resource entry in Terraform state, representing one managed resource or data source and all its instances. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: source
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The module source address, which tells Terraform where to find the module source code. Can be a local path, Terraform Registry address, GitHub URL, or other supported source types.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: postconditions
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type Postcondition Multiplicity 0..* Postconditions that must hold after applying changes to this resource.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: provisioners
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type Provisioner Multiplicity 0..* Provisioner blocks for post-creation actions. Provisioners are a last resort.

references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.title: name
references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The output name.

references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.title: module
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The module path for this resource (e.g., ‘module.vpc’). Empty string for root module resources.

references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.title: indexKey
references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The index key for this instance when using count (numeric) or for_each (string key). Null for single-instance resources.

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: count
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The count meta-argument as an HCL expression.

references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.title: sensitive
references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 When true, marks the variable value as sensitive. Sensitive values are redacted from Terraform output and logs.

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: forEach
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The for_each meta-argument as an HCL expression.

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: variables
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type Variable Multiplicity 0..* Input variable declarations for this module.

references/eClassifiers/DocumentedNamedE.title: description
references/eClassifiers/DocumentedNamedE.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Human-readable description of the element, used for documentation generation.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: preconditions
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type Precondition Multiplicity 0..* Preconditions that must hold before planning changes to this resource.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: lifecycle
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type Lifecycle Multiplicity 0..1 Lifecycle customization for this data source.

references/eClassifiers/NamedElement/ind.title: NamedElement
references/eClassifiers/NamedElement/ind.content: Abstract base class for all named Terraform elements. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Workspace/index..title: Workspace
references/eClassifiers/Workspace/index..content: A named workspace that isolates state data for separate Terraform runs of the same configuration. Each workspace has its own state file. The ‘default’ workspace is created automatically. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.title: privateJson
references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Base64-encoded private state data used by the provider.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: provider
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Optional provider meta-argument that overrides the default provider for this data source.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: arguments
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Resource-type-specific configuration arguments.

references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.title: configurationAlias
references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 An optional alias for this provider requirement when the module needs access to a provider with an alias defined by the calling module.

references/eClassifiers/Backend/referenc.title: type
references/eClassifiers/Backend/referenc.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The backend type (e.g., ‘local’, ‘s3’, ‘azurerm’, ‘gcs’, ‘remote’, ‘http’, ‘consul’, ‘pg’, ‘kubernetes’).

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: type
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The data source type, which is provider-defined (e.g., ‘aws_ami’, ‘azurerm_resource_group’).

references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.title: ignoreChanges
references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* List of attribute paths that Terraform should ignore when planning updates. Changes to these attributes will not trigger a plan diff.

references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.title: value
references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The HCL expression whose result is exported as the output value.

references/eClassifiers/Variable/index.h.title: Variable
references/eClassifiers/Variable/index.h.content: An input variable declaration that allows callers to customize module behavior without modifying the source. Variables are the parameters of Terraform modules, comparable to function arguments in programming. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Output/index.htm.title: Output
references/eClassifiers/Output/index.htm.content: An output value declaration that exposes values from a module to the calling module or to the user. Output values make information about infrastructure available after applying the configuration. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Backend/index.ht.title: Backend
references/eClassifiers/Backend/index.ht.content: A backend block that defines where Terraform stores its state data files. The backend also determines how operations such as plan and apply are executed. Each configuration can have at most one backend block. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/index.title: ModuleCall
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/index.content: A module call (module block) that instantiates a child module. A module is a container for multiple resources that are used together. The module call specifies the source of the module and passes input variable values to it. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.title: ResourceInstance
references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.content: A specific instance of a resource tracked in Terraform state. When using count or for_each, a resource may have multiple instances, each tracked separately. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.title: name
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The resource name as defined in the configuration.

references/eClassifiers/Provider/referen.title: arguments
references/eClassifiers/Provider/referen.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Provider-specific configuration arguments (e.g., region, access_key for AWS).

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: requiredProviders
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type ProviderRequirement Multiplicity 0..* Required provider declarations for this module, specifying provider source addresses and version constraints.

references/eClassifiers/Local/index.html.title: Local
references/eClassifiers/Local/index.html.content: A local value is a convenience feature that assigns a name to an expression, allowing the same expression to be used multiple times without repeating it. Comparable to a local variable in a programming language. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/State/references.title: version
references/eClassifiers/State/references.content: Type EInt Multiplicity 1 The state file format version number.

references/eClassifiers/Precondition/ind.title: Precondition
references/eClassifiers/Precondition/ind.content: A precondition block that verifies assumptions about a resource, data source, or output before applying changes. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Validation/index.title: Validation
references/eClassifiers/Validation/index.content: A validation block within an input variable definition. Validation rules allow module authors to define constraints on accepted variable values. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Postcondition/re.title: errorMessage
references/eClassifiers/Postcondition/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The error message to display if the condition fails.

references/eClassifiers/Postcondition/re.title: condition
references/eClassifiers/Postcondition/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 An HCL expression that must evaluate to true after the operation. If false, Terraform will produce an error.

references/eClassifiers/State/references.title: lineage
references/eClassifiers/State/references.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 A unique identifier for this state file, generated when the state is first created and unchanged thereafter. Used to detect unintended state swaps.

references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.title: sensitiveAttributes
references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 JSON-encoded list of attribute paths that are marked as sensitive in the state.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: postconditions
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type Postcondition Multiplicity 0..* Postconditions that must hold after reading this data source.

references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.title: FILE
references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.content: Copies files or directories from the machine running Terraform to a remote resource.

references/eClassifiers/Postcondition/in.title: Postcondition
references/eClassifiers/Postcondition/in.content: A postcondition block that verifies guarantees about a resource or data source after applying changes. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/State/references.title: terraformVersion
references/eClassifiers/State/references.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The version of Terraform that last wrote this state file.

references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.title: mode
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.content: Type ResourceMode Multiplicity 1 The resource mode: ‘managed’ for resources or ‘data’ for data sources.

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: arguments
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Input variable values passed to the module. Each argument corresponds to an input variable defined in the module.

references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.title: type
references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Type constraint expressed as an HCL type expression (e.g., ‘string’, ‘number’, ‘bool’, ‘list(string)’, ‘map(any)’, ‘object({…})’).

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: forEach
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The for_each meta-argument as an HCL expression.

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: providers
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Provider configuration assignments passed to the module, mapping module-local provider names to parent provider configurations.

references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.title: schemaVersion
references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.content: Type EInt Multiplicity 0..1 The version of the resource schema used to create this state entry.

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: dependsOn
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Explicit dependencies for this module call.

references/eClassifiers/Configuration/in.title: Configuration
references/eClassifiers/Configuration/in.content: A root module configuration that represents a complete Terraform configuration - a directory of .tf files that forms the root module. The Configuration is the main entry point from which Terraform executes and includes backend configuration and provider declarations. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: lifecycle
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type Lifecycle Multiplicity 0..1 Lifecycle customization for this resource.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: preconditions
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type Precondition Multiplicity 0..* Preconditions that must hold before reading this data source.

references/eClassifiers/Validation/refer.title: errorMessage
references/eClassifiers/Validation/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The error message to display when the condition is false. Must be a string value and should describe the constraint.

references/eClassifiers/State/references.title: serial
references/eClassifiers/State/references.content: Type EInt Multiplicity 1 A monotonically increasing integer that is incremented each time Terraform makes a change to state. Used for conflict detection in remote backends.

references/eClassifiers/Precondition/ref.title: condition
references/eClassifiers/Precondition/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 An HCL expression that must evaluate to true. If false, Terraform will produce an error.

references/eClassifiers/Argument/referen.title: value
references/eClassifiers/Argument/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The argument value as an HCL expression string. This may reference variables, locals, resource attributes, or data source attributes.

references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.title: preventDestroy
references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 When true, any attempt to destroy the resource will produce an error. Useful for protecting critical infrastructure components.

references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.title: validations
references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.content: Type Validation Multiplicity 0..* Custom validation rules for the variable value.

references/eClassifiers/Argument/index.h.title: Argument
references/eClassifiers/Argument/index.h.content: An argument assigns a value to a particular name within a Terraform block. Arguments appear within blocks and assign a value to a name. The value can be a literal value or an expression. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: outputs
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type Output Multiplicity 0..* Output value declarations for this module.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: arguments
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Data-source-type-specific filter and configuration arguments.

references/eClassifiers/State/index.html.title: State
references/eClassifiers/State/index.html.content: Terraform state, which is the record of all managed infrastructure and configuration. Terraform uses state to map real-world resources to the configuration, track metadata, and improve performance for large infrastructures. State is stored in a file (terraform.tfstate by default) or in a remote backend. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.title: type
references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The type of the output value.

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: moduleCalls
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type ModuleCall Multiplicity 0..* Child module calls within this module.

references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.title: ProvisionerType
references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.content: Types of provisioners supported by Terraform. Provisioners are used to execute scripts on local or remote machines as part of resource creation or destruction.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: type
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The resource type, which is provider-defined (e.g., ‘aws_instance’, ‘azurerm_virtual_network’). Combined with the name, forms the resource address.

index.html.title: Terraform Model
index.html.content: Ecore model for Terraform concepts based on the Terraform glossary (https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/docs/glossary). The model can be used to: - Generate documentation about Terraform configurations - Generate Terraform configuration artifacts (.tf files) from model instances - Load and represent information from existing Terraform artifacts (configuration files, state files) Postcondition Lifecycle Workspace StateOutput Precondition ProviderRequirement ProvisionerType Local Backend ModuleCall NamedElement Configuration backend Output preconditions Validation DocumentedNamedElement State outputs Argument arguments providers arguments Provisioner arguments Resource postconditions lifecycle preconditions provisioners arguments DataSource postconditions lifecycle preconditions arguments Variable validations Module outputs dataSources variables moduleCalls requiredProviders resources locals StateResource resources ResourceMode ResourceInstance instances Provider arguments providers Kubernetes

references/eClassifiers/Argument/referen.title: name
references/eClassifiers/Argument/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The argument name (left-hand side of the assignment).

references/eClassifiers/Provider/index.h.title: Provider
references/eClassifiers/Provider/index.h.content: A provider block that configures a specific provider plugin. Providers are responsible for understanding API interactions and exposing resources. Each provider name corresponds to a provider declared in required_providers. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: forEach
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The for_each meta-argument as an HCL expression. When set to a map or set, creates one instance per element.

references/eClassifiers/Precondition/ref.title: errorMessage
references/eClassifiers/Precondition/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The error message to display if the condition fails.

references/eClassifiers/Configuration/re.title: backend
references/eClassifiers/Configuration/re.content: Type Backend Multiplicity 0..1 The backend configuration for this root module, defining where state is stored.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: count
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The count meta-argument as an HCL expression.

references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.title: value
references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The output value as a JSON-encoded string.

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: requiredTerraformVersion
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Version constraint string for the minimum Terraform version required by this module.

references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.title: sensitive
references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 When true, marks the output value as sensitive. Sensitive values are redacted from Terraform output and logs.

references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.title: preconditions
references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.content: Type Precondition Multiplicity 0..* Preconditions that must be met before evaluating the output value.

references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.title: type
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The resource type (e.g., ‘aws_instance’).

references/eClassifiers/DocumentedNamedE.title: DocumentedNamedElement
references/eClassifiers/DocumentedNamedE.content: Abstract base class for named Terraform elements that can have a description. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/NamedElement/ref.title: name
references/eClassifiers/NamedElement/ref.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The name of the element as defined in the Terraform configuration.

references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.title: createBeforeDestroy
references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 When true, Terraform will create a new resource before destroying the old one when the resource must be replaced. Useful for zero-downtime deployments.

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: dataSources
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type DataSource Multiplicity 0..* Data sources declared in this module.

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: resources
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type Resource Multiplicity 0..* Managed resources declared in this module.

references/eClassifiers/ResourceMode/ref.title: DATA
references/eClassifiers/ResourceMode/ref.content: A data source that reads existing infrastructure but does not manage its lifecycle.

references/eClassifiers/Validation/refer.title: condition
references/eClassifiers/Validation/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 An HCL expression that returns true if the value is valid. Must return a boolean and may only reference the variable being validated.

references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.title: default
references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Default value for the variable as an HCL expression. If omitted, the caller must provide a value.

references/eClassifiers/Provisioner/refe.title: arguments
references/eClassifiers/Provisioner/refe.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Configuration arguments for the provisioner (e.g., command, interpreter, working_dir for local-exec).

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: dependsOn
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Explicit dependencies for the resource, used when Terraform cannot automatically infer dependencies from expressions. Specified as reference strings (e.g., ‘aws_s3_bucket.example’).

references/eClassifiers/ResourceMode/ind.title: ResourceMode
references/eClassifiers/ResourceMode/ind.content: The mode of a resource in Terraform state. Indicates whether the resource is a managed resource or a data source.

references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.title: nullable
references/eClassifiers/Variable/referen.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 When false, Terraform will reject null values for this variable. Defaults to true.

references/eClassifiers/Module/index.htm.title: Module
references/eClassifiers/Module/index.htm.content: A module is a container for multiple resources that are used together. Every Terraform configuration has at least one module, known as the root module. A module consists of a collection of .tf and/or .tf.json files kept together in a directory. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: provider
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Optional provider meta-argument that overrides the default provider for this resource. Specified as ‘provider_name.alias’ to reference a provider with an alias.

references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.title: attributesJson
references/eClassifiers/ResourceInstance.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 JSON-encoded map of the resource instance’s attribute values as tracked in state.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.title: count
references/eClassifiers/Resource/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The count meta-argument as an HCL expression. When set to a whole number, creates that many instances of the resource.

references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.title: version
references/eClassifiers/ModuleCall/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Version constraint for the module when sourced from the Terraform Registry.

references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/inde.title: StateOutput
references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/inde.content: An output value recorded in Terraform state. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Local/references.title: value
references/eClassifiers/Local/references.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The HCL expression that computes the local value.

references/eClassifiers/Resource/index.h.title: Resource
references/eClassifiers/Resource/index.h.content: A resource block that describes one or more infrastructure objects, such as virtual networks, compute instances, or higher-level components such as DNS records. Resources are the most important element in Terraform; each resource block describes one or more infrastructure objects. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ResourceMode/ref.title: MANAGED
references/eClassifiers/ResourceMode/ref.content: A managed resource whose lifecycle Terraform controls.

references/eClassifiers/Configuration/re.title: providers
references/eClassifiers/Configuration/re.content: Type Provider Multiplicity 0..* Provider configuration blocks for providers used in this configuration.

references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.title: LOCAL_EXEC
references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.content: Executes a command locally on the machine running Terraform.

references/eClassifiers/State/references.title: resources
references/eClassifiers/State/references.content: Type StateResource Multiplicity 0..* All resources tracked in this state.

references/eClassifiers/Provisioner/inde.title: Provisioner
references/eClassifiers/Provisioner/inde.content: A provisioner within a resource block that executes scripts or copies files on a resource after it is created. Provisioners are a last resort and should be avoided when possible in favor of provider-native mechanisms. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/index..title: Lifecycle
references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/index..content: A lifecycle block within a resource that customizes the resource lifecycle behavior. Provides fine-grained control over how Terraform creates, updates, and destroys resources. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.title: source
references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 The provider source address in the format [hostname/]namespace/type, e.g. ‘hashicorp/aws’ or ‘registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/azurerm’.

references/eClassifiers/Provisioner/refe.title: type
references/eClassifiers/Provisioner/refe.content: Type ProvisionerType Multiplicity 1 The type of provisioner: local-exec, remote-exec, or file.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/index.title: DataSource
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/index.content: A data source allows Terraform to use information defined outside of Terraform, defined by another separate Terraform configuration, or modified by functions. Each data source corresponds to a data block in configuration. Data sources read existing infrastructure but do not manage its lifecycle. Diagram

references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.title: replaceTriggeredBy
references/eClassifiers/Lifecycle/refere.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* List of references to resources or resource attributes that, when changed, should trigger replacement of this resource.

references/eClassifiers/State/references.title: outputs
references/eClassifiers/State/references.content: Type StateOutput Multiplicity 0..* Output values recorded in this state.

references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.title: locals
references/eClassifiers/Module/reference.content: Type Local Multiplicity 0..* Local value declarations for this module.

references/eClassifiers/Backend/referenc.title: arguments
references/eClassifiers/Backend/referenc.content: Type Argument Multiplicity 0..* Backend-type-specific configuration arguments (e.g., bucket, key, region for S3 backend).

references/eClassifiers/Provider/referen.title: alias
references/eClassifiers/Provider/referen.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Optional alias for this provider configuration, allowing multiple configurations of the same provider type. Resources can then select a specific alias via the provider meta-argument.

references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.title: instances
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.content: Type ResourceInstance Multiplicity 0..* The resource instances tracked in state.

references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.title: dependsOn
references/eClassifiers/DataSource/refer.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Explicit dependencies for the data source.

references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.title: dependsOn
references/eClassifiers/Output/reference.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..* Explicit dependencies for the output value, used when the value does not implicitly depend on all required resources. Specified as reference strings.

references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.title: version
references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.content: Type EString Multiplicity 0..1 Version constraint string for the provider, e.g. ‘~> 4.0’, ‘>= 3.0, < 5.0’.

references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.title: REMOTE_EXEC
references/eClassifiers/ProvisionerType/.content: Executes commands on the remote resource after it is created.

references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.title: sensitive
references/eClassifiers/StateOutput/refe.content: Type EBoolean Multiplicity 0..1 Whether this output value is marked as sensitive.

references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.title: providerConfig
references/eClassifiers/StateResource/re.content: Type EString Multiplicity 1 The provider configuration address for this resource (e.g., ‘provider[“registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws”]’).

references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.title: ProviderRequirement
references/eClassifiers/ProviderRequirem.content: A required_providers entry that declares a dependency on a Terraform provider. Specifies the source address and version constraints for a provider. Diagram
